parentAction.completionRule: A parametric relevant-pair enumerator/recurrence is recorded, or a generator-source blocker names exactly why the finite restored menu cannot be lifted.

blockedObject.requiredStatement: Enumerate all future or theorem-relevant pairs (n,Q) for the 32 mod 50 lane, where n is a family representative and Q is a square witness modulus that can obstruct c*n+1 for c = 32 + 50*m.
requiredOutputShape: A parametric formula or recurrence for representatives n.
A finite or recursively generated set of possible square witness moduli Q.
For each pair (n,Q), the bad-lane denominator Q/gcd(50*n,Q).
A handoff label proving how the bad lane is avoided, top-tie repaired, contrast-only repaired, or terminally blocked.
blockedObject.blockedReason: The local repo has finite row-menu consumers and finite restored menus, but no source command or theorem object that generates all future family representatives and their possible square witness moduli.

consumerCommands.result: exports theorem bridge from existing report surface

richestFiniteMenuPairSurface.interpretation: This is a finite witness-pair table over the restored menu. It is useful for recurrence mining but does not prove that future rows have no new Q values or new bad-lane classes.

negativeFindings: The local p848-live-frontier path chooses the richest existing menu; it does not generate the menu.
load_best_family_menu_surface keeps representative, known-failure flag, and tuple key for scoring; it is not a theorem-level relevant-pair enumerator.
The seed-bundle manifests preserve family-menu summary counts and candidate scores, but not raw parametric row generation.
No local CLI command found in this audit emits a recurrence for all future representatives n or all future square witness moduli Q.

finiteEvidenceUsableNext.reason: The restored menus from NINETEEN through TWENTY_FOUR provide a finite row-count sequence and a finite Q surface. They can be mined for a stability/recurrence candidate, but cannot by themselves discharge the all-future quantifier.
finiteEvidenceUsableNext.sequenceGap: The local restored sequence jumps from 260 to 280 rows; the missing 270-row menu or the generating recurrence is not present as a local source.

deterministicBoundary.blockedInput: A local generator, recurrence, or theorem that emits every future relevant (n,Q) pair for c = 32 + 50*m.
acceptedSuccessors: A generator-source restoration packet that names the command and reproduces the finite menus.
A finite-menu sequence recurrence/stability packet proving or falsifying a candidate row generator from the restored 220..280 row menus.
A concrete residual counterfamily with explicit n, Q, m, denominator Q/gcd(50*n,Q), and failed handoff.
deterministicBoundary.forbiddenShortcut: Do not promote the finite 280-row menu or the 139 lane-32 witness repair rows to a universal domain-cover theorem without a row generator, finite Q partition, or recurrence proof.

nextTheoremMove: Mine the restored SIX_PREFIX_NINETEEN through TWENTY_FOUR menu sequence for a stable relevant-pair recurrence or Q-partition candidate; if no recurrence survives, emit a sequence-stability blocker before any 40501+ rollout.
suggestedVerificationCommand: jq '{blockedObject, localAudit, finiteEvidenceUsableNext, deterministicBoundary, recommendedNextAction, claims}' packs/number-theory/problems/848/SPLIT_ATOM_PACKETS/FRONTIER_BRIDGE/P848_MOD50_RELEVANT_PAIR_ENUMERATOR_GENERATOR_BLOCKER_PACKET.json
proofBoundary: This packet blocks the parametric mod-50 relevant-pair enumerator because the local sources only consume finite menus. It does not prove a universal square-witness cover, finite Q partition, contrast-only recombination, p4217 complement coverage, or all-N.
